chapter_title.ar: بَابُ مَنْ قَدَّمَ شَيْئاً أَوْ أَخَّرَهُ مِنْ مَنَاسِكِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 189 – The one who brings forward or delays something from his rituals

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/191)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/191)
text: رَجُلٍ زَارَ الْبَيْتَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ زَارَ الْبَيْتَ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَحْلِقَ وَ هُوَ عَالِمٌ أَنَّ ذَلِكَ لَا يَنْبَغِي لَهُ فَإِنَّ عَلَيْهِ دَمَ شَاةٍ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Sahl Bi Ziyad altogether, from Ibn Mahbou, from Abu Ayoub Al Khazzaz, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who visited the House (Kabah) before he had shaved off (his head). So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If he had visited the House (Kabah) before he had shaved off (his head), and he was knowing that, that was not befitting for him, so upon him is blood of a sheep (as an expiation)’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad and Sahl ibn Ziyad all ibn Mahbub from abu Ayyub al-Khazzaz from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “This is about a man who visits the House before shaving. Abu Ja’far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If he has knowingly visited the House before shaving, it is not proper, thus he owes one sheep.’”
